response.justification: Bogdan has genuine geospatial domain exposure through GISCollective, Green Map GIS work, and a FOSS4G 2019 talk, and his time at Sociomantic demonstrates comfort with high-throughput distributed systems. However, the role's primary technical requirement — modern C++ or Rust in a production engineering context — is essentially absent from his profile. His C++ usage (NI LabWindows, 2014–2015; a bachelor's thesis audio project) is over a decade old and narrow in scope. His current and recent engineering output is entirely TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, with no Rust whatsoever and no experience with routing tiles, navigation pipelines, or map data transformation at any level of seniority.
strengths: Geospatial domain familiarity via GISCollective, Green Map GIS platform development, and a FOSS4G 2019 conference talk — directly relevant industry context
Distributed systems exposure at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, async I/O DLang services
Strong software craftsmanship culture (TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD) and a demonstrated history of picking up unfamiliar technology stacks
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and over 10 years stale (NI LabWindows, 2014–2015; a thesis audio project); no Rust experience at all — the role's core language requirement is unmet
No routing, navigation guidance, or routing-tile pipeline experience; geospatial work is limited to GIS data collection and map visualization, not routing optimization or tiling
Current and recent engineering is almost entirely TypeScript/Node.js, representing a substantial language and domain gap requiring significant ramp-up for a mid-senior IC role

key_factors.factor: No routing or navigation pipeline experience
